text.title: DR Drill Scenario Library Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the DR Drill Scenario Library methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: DR library MUST contain at least 6 scenarios covering: region loss, db logical corruption, secret-store loss, identity provider outage, vendor breach, ransomware. Single-scenario libraries are rejected.
rule.rationale: Real disasters are not 'last drill again'; library coverage forces the team to practice uncommon scenarios.

rule.statement: Each scenario MUST have objective declaration criteria (metric, log pattern, vendor alert). Subjective declaration ('feels bad') is rejected.
rule.rationale: Subjective declaration causes drills to drift to 'familiar' incidents; objective criteria force the right scenario.

rule.statement: Each scenario MUST include a restore-validation step with explicit pass criteria (data integrity check, transaction replay, user-facing smoke test). 'Service is up' alone is not validation.
rule.rationale: Services come up with empty DBs; data integrity is the real recovery metric.

rule.statement: Scenarios MUST rotate on a published calendar (one scenario per quarter minimum). The same scenario MUST NOT run two quarters in a row.
rule.rationale: Rotation prevents the team from over-rehearsing one scenario and under-preparing for others.

rule.statement: Every drill produces a post-mortem within 7 days, regardless of outcome. Post-mortem references the scenario id + lists gaps + assigns gap-fix tickets with owners.
rule.rationale: Without post-mortem, drills become check-the-box; the gap is the value.

rule.statement: Library MUST include at least one scenario where a primary vendor (cloud, CDN, identity provider, payment processor) is unavailable for >4h. Drill exercises the actual fallback path.
rule.rationale: Vendor outages are inevitable; teams that drill cloud-only-as-self learn nothing about vendor dependence.
